name: velocity_just_under_threshold_no_arm
description: velocity is strictly less than threshold by an epsilon-scale gap. Pins the exclusive side of the boundary. Chosen so the subtraction is exact in IEEE-754 binary (0.5 - 0.0625 = 0.4375, both exactly representable) -- no float-noise excuse for a wrong impl that flips the comparison.
derivation: velocity = current_return - prev_return = 0.4375 - 0.0 = 0.4375. should_arm = (velocity >= para_threshold) AND (not currently_armed) = (0.4375 >= 0.5) AND (not False) = False AND True = False. 0.4375 is exactly representable in binary float (7 * 2^-4), so the subtraction is exact.
